entry.title: Re: Clean Core maturity and the new Extensibility Levels
entry.content:
@EmilianoAyub @wagener-mark 

We are in tight situation that we cannot change IDOC to APIs considering several dimensions and dependencies. Since existing SAP ECC have some custom fields for few partners and we don't wanted to disturb existing mappings in middleware.

As is -   200 + EDI Partners <-> MS Biztalk  <-> SAP ECC

To be - 200 + EDI Partners <-> MS Biztalk  <-> SAP S4 - Utilize same enchanced IDOC to minizmei code changes in biztalk for faster delivery

Also is it possible to enhance IDOCs in SAP BTP? like :

200 + EDI Partners <-> MS Biztalk  <-> SAP BTP(idoc extensions) <-> SAP S4 (clean core)

Thankyou!!

entry.title: 1010 Balance Carryforward - Divestured Consolidation Unit's Reserves are not BCF
entry.content:
After consolidation unit has been divested, its reserves are maintained because they represent realized profits that increased the group's equity and, upon consolidation, became part of consolidated equity, regardless of the fact that the subsidiary no longer belongs to the group.

But the BCF Balance Carryforward task at SAP GR Data Monitor task is not taking into account these reserves at the beginning balance of the following year.

Are we missing any any automatic task or setup that manages this point?

If the answer is no, please would you mind to let us know which are SAP best practices for this point.

entry.title: Is there any costing validation at process order creation before save?
entry.content:
Hope everyone aware that we can set the erro message on process/ Production order if there are costing issue. 

1. Error message "Profit Center not maintained Order xxx” - often message KO 438 – OBA5, OBMSG 
2. Error messages in the costing log “No price could be determined for a material /batch xxx plant yyy” CK 465 - OPR4_PPC

3. Reject release if the status Costed with Errors (CSER)? BS22
But this validation is not working at creation stage COR1/CO01.  Consider I create a process order and first release it and then save it. The above validation is based on costing. But the costing is done only at saving the order. By the time I have released the order. Is there any validation which either do the costing / set the status before release on CR01/CO01 transaction.


May say process order to be converted from planned order. But in case of rework order how it will work?

entry.title: process hierarchy node to a process in Cloud ALM
entry.content: hi, When I assign a process hierarchy node to a process in Cloud ALM and then open the process hierarchy app, I unfortunately don't see the assigned processes under the process hierarchy. Therefore, I don't have a complete overview of which process belongs to which process hierarchy node.

entry.title: Using local (Belgian) G/L account numbers vs. YCOA: what’s the exact rule across apps?
entry.content:
Hi all,

We run S/4HANA Public Cloud with YCOA as the operational CoA. We want to work with local (Belgian) G/L numbers for day‑to‑day use (at least for entry/display), while accepting YCOA as the technical posting account.

My understanding:

  • In FI apps where users manually select a G/L, local/alternative accounts can be used for selection/display (e.g., Post General Journal Entries via Edit Options for Journal Entries → Alternative Account).
  • In system-determined scenarios (MM-driven postings, AP/AR operational apps, uploads/APIs), postings run on YCOA; local numbers may appear in value help but are not the operational posting account.
  • Example: In Create Supplier Invoice, Offsetting Account value help shows both local and YCOA numbers, but after selection the app posts/displays YCOA.

Questions:
Could you please provide a concise list or rule-of-thumb matrix of where local/alternative accounts are usable (entry/display) and where they are not in Public Cloud—beyond Post General Journal Entries (e.g., accruals, reclassification, asset postings without PO, etc.)? Representative examples are sufficient; no need for an exhaustive tile-by-tile list.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: DESIGN SAP SYSTEM FOR 6 REMOTE, 1 CETRAL SYSTEM
entry.content:
Good Afternoon

Hope This The The Correct Platform.

I Have Been Tasked To Create An Archetecture Document For A SAP System Consisting Of 6 Remote (Off-Shore) And 1 Central (On-Shore) System. The 6 Systems  Need To Be Self Contained And Connectable To Two Other Off-Systems (For DR Recovery) On Site Access By Human Not Possible.

My Thoughts Are 7 SAP S4/HANA On-Premise SAP Systems, Any Help Or Comment Appreciated

Thanks Neil

entry.title: Re: Facing an issue with BTP CAP application deployment -...
entry.content: Maybe this will help: We have the same problem. Circumstances: Both develeopers use BAS. Developer 1 deploys from their workspace, developer 2 tries to redeploy the same project (synchronized via GitHub) from their workspace later. Developer 2 gets the error, developer 1 does not. Developer 1 originally created the project. Cloud Foundry logins and permissions are all OK.

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Invoicing (formerly SAP Ariba Central Invoi...
entry.content:
Yes, from the 2602 (Feb 14th) release, SAP Ariba Invoicing (formerly SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management), SKU 8018575, is available for our S/4HANA Private Cloud, On Prem (2022 sp1 or later), SAP ERP (ECC 6 EHP 8 or better) customers (in addition to S/4HANA Public Cloud).
The same SKU and functionality applies to all SAP ERP's. SAP Ariba Invoicing should be positioned as a central service, connected to one or many SAP ERP's (of the same or different types). The metric is total #of invoices processed and replicated from the ERP in blocks of 1,000 invoices/year. Min block size is 20. 

Note: there may be some regional limitations in regards to availability in the price list. Please go to the DE or US pricelist if you cannot find it. We've asked pricing to update to availability in alignment with localizations available (30+ to date).

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1 is now available.

To access the final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer. In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access it.

For you as a customer, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool. The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

You can use the commenting feature for What’s New. With this feature, authors can evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: Re: Facing an issue with BTP CAP application deployment -...
entry.content:
Maybe this will help:

We have the same problem. Circumstances:

Both develeopers use BAS.

Developer 1 deploys from their workspace, developer 2 tries to redeploy the same project (synchronized via GitHub) from their workspace later.
Developer 2 gets the error, developer 1 does not.

Developer 1 originally created the project. Cloud Foundry logins and permissions are all OK.

But: the project was deployed by dev. 2!
Best regards
Adalbert

Janos

entry.title: Store EAN number at settlement document item level for reporting purpose
entry.content:
Hi experts @Lakshmipathi  @GauthierVictor 

We have a requirement to include EAN No. (18 Char) number against each line item in the settlement document. This EAN number comes from material determination setup.

Do we have any standard field where we can store this EAN no. so that we can use this EAN no. for report purpose and other purpose. I have seen a field supplier material number at item level (shown in the attached screenshot) can we use this field, i have tried to put some number in the field in manual rebate process to show you but we are using only automatic settlement only (WB2R_SC). Or do we have any other field we have to store this EAN number, Kindly suggest.

 

Settlement Management  SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Rescheduling of maintenance orders after breakdown
entry.content:
Greetings,

only technical completion of the current call object affects the dates of future objects. This is controled by:

  • Shift Factor Late Completion
  • Shift Factor Early Completion

This might be a bit tricky with your scenario since planned call object execution date corresponds with next call object creation. I think to acheive the desired behaviour you would need to also select the "Completion Requirement" indicator. This indicator defines that no call object is created if previous one is not complteed

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements and "Unsupported OData Version 4.01"
entry.content:
@jensittel yes that is true.
In my case i am dependend on a lower Version of SAPUI5, because its a S/4HANA 2023 FP02 On Prem and i don't to manually set a SAPUI5 Version in the index.html to a specific version of a not stable version.

So if someone still has the problem in a deployed version:

  • Check if your Service Binding exposes an entity with a field thats form type decfloat.
    • If so, cast it to a decimal value.
  • In the SAPUI5 App, change the OData Version of the service in your datasources inside manifest.json back to 4.0 instead of 4.01.
    It does not get changed automatically if you refresh the metadata via the Service Manager (Fiori Tools)
  • re-deploy and test

entry.title: Re: Unsupported OData version 4.01
entry.content:
So if someone still has the problem in a deployed version:

  • Check if your Service Binding exposes an entity with a field thats form type decfloat.
    • If so, cast it to a decimal value.
  • In the SAPUI5 App, change the OData Version of the service in your datasources inside manifest.json back to 4.0 instead of 4.01.
    It does not get changed automatically if you refresh the metadata via the Service Manager (Fiori Tools)
  • re-deploy and test

entry.title: Re: Unsupported OData version 4.01
entry.content: @jensittel yes that is true. In my case i am dependend on a lower Version of SAPUI5, because its a S/4HANA 2023 FP02 On Prem and i don't to manually set a SAPUI5 Version in the index.html to a specific version of a not stable version. So if someone still has the problem in a deployed version: Check if your Service Binding exposes an entity with a field thats form type decfloat. If so, cast it to a decimal value. In the SAPUI5 App, change the OData Version of the service in your datasources inside manifest.json back to 4.0 instead of 4.01. It does not get changed automatically if you refresh the metadata via the Service Manager (Fiori Tools) re-deploy and test

entry.title: Re: Store EAN number at settlement document item level fo...
entry.content:
Hi @kirankumardara, 

You already have part of the answer, indeed.

At this stage, there is no standard field for EAN available in the settlement document line item. However, you still have two possible options:

  • Append the WBRP table — preferably via WBRP_INCL_EEW_PS and all other relevant related tables, depending on your design (for example split criteria structures, communication structures, etc.)

  • Repurpose an existing field, if a suitable unused field is available in your setup

In a second step, if you want this field to be populated automatically, the easiest approach would be to leverage the split criteria in the Business Volume Profile, so that the value is transferred from business volume into the settlement document line item.

Regards, 
Gauthier VICTOR

entry.title: Re: Failed to resolve navigation target "#Bank-manageExtended"

entry.title: Re: SLT Replication Error: Syntax error in runtime object – Missing IUUC_OPERAT_FLAG for CDS View
entry.content:
Hello Maciej,

A couple things this could be, if you have a rule assignment that is calling the IUUC_OPERAT_FLAG field from the logging table the include may not have the correct syntax. Another issue could be if you have performed a refresh and the refresh guide was not followed https://help.sap.com/doc/6a30dd8845174864a0ee77c21f2fcbbf/3.0.14/en-US/Resuming_Replication.pdf. It may be best to open a case for this in component CA-LT-SLT and we can check from support.

Michael
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025
entry.content:
Thanks @CopperTweet769975 . We were able to resolve this today finally . Also, as we are ECS customers, we had to raise a Service Request to SAP to whitelist the domain. After that was done , as per the response on the Service Request, we had to maintain the below parameters additionally in APPLDESTCC

proxy host: proxy

proxy service: 3128

Once this was done , the error in connection test which was 'Error while receiving client' all this while , changed to 'Status Code: 404 Reason: Not Found' . This meant that now the Network failures were resolved. Post that , was able to register the OTP via program AIC_ADT_PROXY_CERTIFICATE

Rahul

entry.title: Re: Failed to resolve navigation target "#Bank-manageExtended"
entry.content: It depends on your current backed release. Ensure you have selected your current Hana version on the right to see if it's still current for your release version. In this example it's valid until 2022 FPS02 and lower. If it's depreciated on your system release, it is no longer supported and replaced by a different application. Search for "Manage Bank" and choose the most suitable replacement for your current requirements.

entry.title: Unable to purchase additional Learning Hub subscriptions in SAPforMe and SAP Store
entry.content:
We are currently unable to purchase additional Learning Hub subscriptions for our consultants via SAPforMe in the Cloud Contracts area.  I have also tried going directly to the SAP Store and purchasing them (see attached screenshot), but get this error when attempting to submit the order:  The referenced contract item is outdated. Please remove the item from your cart, go back to SAP for Me and add it again. (Error Code: VAL_0028)

Per SAP Note 3318975 this is a known issue that was reported on 9 March.  I created a support request for the SAP Store back on 6 March with no response.  I've also created a support ticket via SAPforMe, but no update either.  This is the second time in a month that I've had issues with Learning Hub subscription purchasing.

Reaching out to the community to see if anyone has found a work around for this.  I have consultants waiting and need the subscription to complete Certification Exams for active customer projects.

Thanks!

entry.title: Tax rate not calculating for intergration with vertex tax engine
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am testing Vertex tax integration for Sales Scheduling Agreements in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

We have two finished product (FERT) materials with similar attributes:

Material A – created manually by the procurement/MM team

Material B – created via data migration templates by the Data Migration team

When I create a Scheduling Agreement with Tax Category O1:

For Material A, the system determines tax correctly and returns 6% in condition type XR1 in the Conditions tab.

For Material B, the XR1 condition is empty, and no tax percentage is determined.

To further verify, I duplicated the manually created material (for example, Material 69) and created a new material (699) with the same attributes. When I used this duplicated material in the Scheduling Agreement, the system again returned 6% in XR1.

However, when using the data-migrated material, the XR1 condition remains empty.

I also checked the Vertex payload, where the material is marked as non-taxable, and therefore no tax percentage is returned.

I suspect that there might be some field differences between the manually created material and the migrated material, as the migrated material was created using data templates and some fields related to tax determination might have been missed.

Could you please advise:

What could cause a migrated material to be treated as non-taxable by Vertex even though it appears to have similar attributes?

Which material fields are required for tax determination when using the Vertex tax engine?

Any suggestions or guidance would be greatly appreciated.


Left one is payload for manaually created material.
Right one is payload for material created using data migration.

Thanks.

on Ai core i have deployed foundation-models Version 0.0.1 for gpt

but i dont have any models under ML Operations, only deployed models..

Im on abap BTP cloud...

entry.title: SO is not created in Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer (5HP)
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

We are using Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer process. In material master availability check "SP" is maintained in receiving plant. When PO is created no SO is created in issuing plant due to no stock. 

The process is issuing plant will start the production most of the times, after PO is created.

Can you suggest possible solution so that SO can be created in issuing plant with Stock of material.

Thanks

Pavan

entry.title: Re: Payroll Control Center - Systems Requirement Minimum

entry.title: Modify HES value with framework agreement
entry.content: Hello community, I need to be able to modify the value of the HES (Hourly Service Evaluation) that comes from a purchase order with a framework agreement. For example, I have a security guard service where 10 guards were contracted but only 9 arrived, so I need to be able to modify the value in the HES.

entry.title: Ready to build AI that changes everything? Are you prepared to design next‑level AI?
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence is evolving fast—but how we design AI matters just as much as the technology itself. If you’re passionate about building impactful, responsible, and user‑centric AI solutions within the SAP ecosystem, there’s a standout course now available on SAP Learning: 👉 Designing Agentic Systems with a Human-Centered Approach

This course teaches you how to co‑design AI with humans in mind, applying proven design principles, responsible AI practices, and practical frameworks to create solutions that are intuitive, safe, ethical, and valuable.

Whether you work in product management, UX, consulting, development, or innovation, you’ll learn how to:

  • Map real business scenarios where agentic AI adds value
  • Integrate human-centered design into every stage of AI solutioning
  • Understand how agents think, decide, and act in SAP contexts
  • Apply responsible AI guidelines to ensure safe and ethical outcomes
  • Prototype AI-driven agents aligned with user needs and workflows

This learning experience is perfect for:

  • SAP consultants and architects
  • Solution and product designers
  • Innovation, AI, and UX practitioners
  • Developers exploring agent-based AI
  • Anyone driving digital transformation initiatives

No heavy technical background required—just curiosity and a passion for designing meaningful AI experiences.
This course equips you with the mindset and methods to design agentic systems responsibly, creatively, and effectively—skills increasingly sought after across industries. 
Take the next step in your AI learning journey—become a designer of AI that truly works for people.
🔗 Start Learning Now 👉 Enroll here:

entry.title: Re: Is there a good way to automatically export a printed...
entry.content:
Hi Fiona,

We are an SAP Partner , APOS Systems, and we have a solution called Publisher for Cloud. 
This solution offers the ability to burst and broadcast SAC stories using dynamic rules for exportation. 

In your situation we would be able to setup 1 single schedule (if you wanted it to happen on a recurring basis, or triggered by events or just on an ad-hoc basis) and dynamically assign those filter values. So 1 schedule would generate all the printed documents with various filter combinations that you need, with ease. 

There are many other useful features that would benefit in your situation as well that help you overcome your challenges, and it's fully automated. 

 

Let me know if this is of interest and I can send you some information for you to review. 

 

Thanks,
Alan

entry.title: Re: MRP Planned Orders Dates & Detailed Material coverage
entry.content:
1. Planned Orders with Past Requirement Dates
You are correct that even if opening dates are maintained, MRP in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not propose dates in the past. This seems to be standard system behavior. Once a requirement date has passed, the system assumes immediate action is required and sets the planned order date to the current date. To help planners prioritize, it is recommended to use exception messages such as “Reschedule In” or “Start Date in the Past.” These messages highlight overdue orders and can be monitored in the Monitor Material Coverage app or MRP Cockpit. This provides visibility into which orders are late and should be prioritized.

2. Time-Phased Overview (MD04 Equivalent)
Regarding the CDS views mentioned earlier (I_MaterialStockRequirement and I_MaterialCoverage), seems these are not released in the Public Cloud edition. They are available in on-premise/private cloud systems but not exposed in Public Cloud. In Public Cloud, the closest standard apps could be (you have to explore as I don't have access to system):

  • Monitor Material Coverage (shows shortages and exception messages)
  • Manage Material Coverage (provides more detail per material)

For a complete time-phased overview similar to MD04, it is recommended to build a custom analytical report using released CDS views such as I_MaterialInventory, I_MaterialRequirement, and I_PurchaseOrderItem. These can be combined to provide a consolidated view of requirements, stock, and shortages across multiple materials, avoiding the need to check product by product.

entry.title: Accessing Your Financial Data: G/L Account Line Items API
entry.content:
If you're working with SAP's Finance module, the G/L Account Line Items - Read (A2X) API offers a straightforward way to retrieve journal entry information. This service pulls data from the universal journal, giving you access to key accounting fields alongside cost accounting objects.

What You Can Retrieve

The API allows you to access balances across multiple areas including General Ledger Accounting, Asset Accounting, Management Accounting, Margin Analysis, and Material Ledger. You can pull three types of balances:

  • Opening balances show totals from the fiscal year start date up to your selected date, including balance carryforward.
  • Moving balances capture all line items posted within a specific time interval, excluding carried-forward balances—useful for analyzing quarterly movements or custom time periods.
  • Ending balances combine opening balances with changes (credits and debits) within your selected timeframe.

Important Limitations

This API isn't designed for full data extraction. The universal journal contains enormous data volumes, so the service exposes only a subset of fields. We recommend using aggregation and applying strong filters to your requests.

If you need line-item level detail, include the following key fields, for example,

  • AccountingDocument,

  • CompanyCode,

  • FiscalYear,

  • Ledger,

  • LedgerGLLineItem, (ACDOCA line item number)

  • SourceLedger,

Note that AccountingDocumentItem (ACDOCA-BUZEI) is only used for associations to BSEG views.

The API also cannot retrieve cumulative ending balances.

You'll find this service on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, where you can explore example requests and additional documentation to help you get started with your financial data queries. The image shows the API on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

Here's where you find additional useful information:

G/L Account Line Items - Read (A2X)

Communication Management

 APIs in Finance

APIs for General Ledger Accounting

entry.title: Re: Automating Probation Period Extension Using Intelligent Services Center in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content: Great blog and informative!! @RISHIKA_R

entry.title: The Universal Documents (Total) button is not registering
entry.content:
When executing the document release process in GR, the Universal Documents (Total) button is not being accounted for. When pressing this button, the documents are updated, but the information is not saved and therefore does not appear in the reports.

The Universal Documents (Total) button is not registering

entry.title: Re: Enable Offline Store for Online OData Service in SAP MDK
entry.content: By checking the Enable Offline Store option this triggers the MDK client to create the offline db store when calling the Initialize Action and specifying your defining requests.   SSAM already uses offline by default but also has an online service for certain functions.

entry.title: Financial Statement Version for Profit & Loss Format in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are currently implementing SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud (Release 2023) and need to configure a Profit & Loss report format using Financial Statement Version (FSV).

I am new to SAP S/4HANA and have already reviewed the available SAP Help documentation and notes, but I would appreciate guidance from experts on the best approach to structure the FSV for P&L reporting.

I will also attach a sample format with dummy data below image for reference that we need to replicate in the system.

Could someone please guide on the recommended format or share any relevant notes or examples?

Gross Profit and Operating Profit are calculated figures – how can these be replicated or configured in FSV?

Same like Balance Sheet report – Profit/Period is a calculated figure – how can this be represented in FSV?

 

 

Thank you for your support.

 

 

Best regards,

silambu

entry.title: Re: SO is not created in Advanced Intercompany Stock Tran...
entry.content: The most robust solution is to enable sales order creation even without stock by adjusting availability check or leveraging aATP backorder processing. This ensures the issuing plant always sees the demand and can start production, rather than blocking the sales order creation. If your customer insists on automatic linkage, aATP is the recommended approach in Public Cloud. The key is to use a checking group and scope of check that allow “negative confirmation” so the sales order is created despite shortages, and then let MRP or aATP trigger production.

entry.title: Ready to simplify global trade with confidence?
entry.content:
Global trade doesn’t have to be complicated.
With the our learning materials you will be able to use the basic functions of SAP Global Trade Services in international trade and to describe the integration of these functions into logistics in SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA. 
I’m excited to highlight a course many of you will instantly recognize as a must‑have foundation:
👉Discovering the Basics of SAP Global Trade Services

This course gives you a clear, friendly introduction to how organizations use SAP Global Trade Management to:

  • Automate import/export tasks
  • Simplify compliance checks
  • Reduce manual work & trade risks
  • Stay aligned with global regulations
  • Keep operations moving—fast

Whether you're exploring global trade for the first time or strengthening your SAP skill set, this course is a perfect entry point for:

✔ SAP newcomers
✔ Logistics & supply chain professionals
✔ Compliance teams
✔ Aspiring SAP consultants
✔ Curious learners in our group 

Jump in, level up, and let’s keep growing together as a learning community!

entry.title: Re: SO is not created in Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer (5HP)
entry.content: Hi! In intercompany scenarios like this, the issue often comes down to how availability check and planning logic are configured between the issuing and receiving plants. Sometimes companies handle this by adjusting the checking rules or by triggering production planning earlier so the sales order can still be generated even when stock isn’t currently available. More broadly, a lot of organizations are also looking at ways to streamline these types of cross-system workflows through automation. I recently read an article about AI call agents and business process automation that touches on how companies are starting to automate complex operational processes, which can also apply to supply chain coordination like this.

entry.title: Re: Custom CDS Views / Analytical Queries Not Visible in...
entry.content:
Quick update guys, i solved the preplem. 

Mainly the solution was to create an  InA service , then create a multidimensional report, 

i followed this SAP Note 2710858  : Custom Analytical Queries created in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition are not visible in SAP Analytics Cloud

Best of luck ! 

Wahiba

entry.title: Re: Planned Order Showing External Procurement Even Thoug...
entry.content: Even though your material master shows procurement type “E – In-house,” external procurement can be triggered by quota arrangements, source lists, or MRP group customizing. Start by checking quota arrangements and source lists as these are the most likely culprits.

entry.title: Re: Week 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account enabled with Joule for Developers
entry.content: Package: ZAC_162143

Date: May 2026, flexible in dates.

Time: Flesible as well.

Audience: We could accommodate up to 50 participants.

Contact: max.vandam@rojoconsultancy.com

Thanks you! 

Kind regards
Max

entry.title: Re: Mirror, Mirror - Programming a Magic Mirror in Snap!
entry.content: Thank you for sharing this, Jadga!

entry.title: Re: Manage Purchase Contracts App – Output Details Greyed...
entry.content:
Hello @badli ,

i have found that in Public Cloud printing XML works best in GUI applications, for example app Change Purchase Contract (instead of Manage Purchase Contracts).

Please see below an explanation of how to access the XML:

1. Search for Purchase Contract and open it via app Change Purchase Contract.

2. Go to Messages.

3. Via "Add Output" you can add a new line as PRINT – important here: you need to fill the field "Form Template" otherwise it won't work.

4. Double-clicking on this new line will open the output where you can add Print Queue #&&DOWNLOAD_FORM_DATA_XML&&#.

5. Accept message (if it pops up).

6. Go back into the overview via the arrow.

7. In the output list, you can now choose "Display Document".

8. The XML will appear and may be downloaded.

 

I hope this helps 🙂

entry.title: Are you ready to master the secrets behind global trade classifications?
entry.content:
If you work with international trade, logistics, or SAP, this course is a must‑learn!
Discover how to configure product classifications that keep global trade moving smoothly and compliantly. 
👉 Configuring and Using Classifications for International Trade

This course helps you understand and configure the classification processes that global companies rely on to keep trade flowing. You get hands-on knowledge to:

  • Configure classifications step by step
  • Understand how classification impacts customs & compliance
  • Connect classification data to trade processes
  • Support audits and reduce manual work
  • Build a solid foundation for SAP GTS, compliance, and logistics roles

It’s the perfect mix of practical, relevant, and career‑boosting content.
This course is tailor‑made for:

  • SAP beginners exploring international trade
  • Trade compliance professionals
  • Logistics and supply chain specialists
  • SAP functional consultants
  • Curious learners in our community looking to level up

No deep technical expertise needed—just your willingness to learn and grow.

This is your chance to gain a skill that truly matters in the real world—one that opens doors across industries.
👉 Explore the course here

entry.title: EBF 8360 Reposted After 11 Days
entry.content:
SQL Anywhere EBF 8360 for Windows x64 was first posted on February 27th but then reposted on March 10th.

https://me.sap.com/notes/2381119 was last updated on March 2nd, still referring to the February 27th release.

SAP Note/KBA 3718731 is not up at all.

The only difference I can see is a change to the SIGNATURE.SMF inside the downloaded ZIP file.

I've got the original February 27th "version" installed already. Do I need to do anything? Why did the date and the signature change but nothing else?

entry.title: CREATE OR REPLACE TYPE
entry.content:
How do I create or replace a type in HANA 2.0 SP5?

I want to create a type if it currently does not exist. How do I do that?

entry.title: Re: Flexible Programming Model Explorer - Building Block...
entry.content:
Hi @VigneshMP , I also didn't have support for this UI5 version, but it worked when I used the object from "sap.ui.fl.variants".

xmlns:fl="sap.ui.fl.variants"

<fl:VariantManagement id="idGlobalVariantManagement" for="idMainFilterBar, idMainTable" />

Regards

entry.title: Data Inconsistency in Manage Dashboard - Timezone/Sync Issue
entry.content:
We are facing a data inconsistency issue in the Manage Dashboards. The dashboard is displaying incorrect timestamps for SFC completion when compared to the official SFC Report.

Analysis: There is an offset of 4 hours between the actual completion time and the dashboard display. It appears the dashboard is not correctly converting the UTC time to the local timezone (Brazil/West - UTC-4), or it is incorrectly assuming a different timezone reference.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Data Inconsistency in Manage Dashboard - Timezone/Syn...
entry.content:
You can utilize below mentioned time zone identifier to convert UTC to Brazilian time zone in eSAC Query Designer 
1. Brazil/Acre
2. Brazil/DeNoronha
3. Brazil/East
4. Brazil/West
 
Below, we have included a sample formula to help you with this conversion:
UTCTOLOCAL(<INPUT_UTC_TIMESTMAP>, 'Brazil/Acre')
UTCTOLOCAL(<INPUT_UTC_TIMESTMAP>, 'Brazil/DeNoronha')
UTCTOLOCAL(<INPUT_UTC_TIMESTMAP>, 'Brazil/East')
UTCTOLOCAL(<INPUT_UTC_TIMESTMAP>, 'Brazil/West'
 
This function will allow you to accurately convert the given UTC timestamp to the respective Brazilian Time Zone.

Please have a look at attached KBs below in regards to UTCTOLOCAL function. This function even though in SAC is basically HANA function.

1791342 - Time Zone Support in SAP HANA - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Week 2 : Use Joule Chat to create ABAP RAP Application and explore the Explain feature
entry.content: Ignore this Comment

entry.title: How to disable the “Change” button in Sales Orders for Sales Representatives in S/4HANA Cloud Public
entry.content:
In addition to a previous request regarding price and condition visibility, we also need to disable the “Change” button in Sales Orders for our team of sales representatives and sales assistants.

The reason is that allowing these users to modify an existing sales order is highly risky. Once a sales order is already being processed by the Production team, any modification performed by Sales may lead to inconsistencies between the order and what has already been produced.

Requirement:
We need a way to restrict access to the Change function of Sales Orders, while still allowing these users to Create and Display sales orders.

Question:
What is the recommended approach in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to disable or restrict the Change option for Sales Orders?
Should this be handled through Business Roles, Restriction Types, IAM App Variants, or another method?

Any guidance or best‑practice recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Week 2 : Use Joule Chat to create ABAP RAP Application and explore the Explain feature
entry.content:
Week 2 Challenge Completed.
Package : ZAC_1893129

entry.title: Employee history eligibility rule to consider termination reasons
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a requirement to include employees who are active and Terminated employees within EC integrated Variable Pay template.

Each country has a separate set of eligible Termination reasons. System should also check the "Date of Termination", It should be within 01/01/2026 and 12/31/2026.

Issue is that if an employee is terminated on 31st March 2026 with a non eligible termination reason or eligible termination reason, System is still pulling the Active record from 1st Jan till 30th March,2026.

Earlier I tried restricting using Manager form eligibility rule. However the issue with that approach is if the "Termination reason" is changed after the forms have been launched, he is not getting removed or greyed out on the Worksheet.

Please let me know how to proceed further.

Regards,

Krishna.

We have a ticket where SAP confirmed that issue and answered: "The corresponding corrections are planned to be delivered with HFC06 or HFC07, with the release expected by the end of March."

entry.title: Re: Can the system notify managers when a team member rec...
entry.content: Thank you so much for your help, Nicolas!

entry.title: Re: Value Help Restrictions in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud –...
entry.content:
Dear @DavidBirrer,

The standard behavior is closer to Option B.

SAP documents Read, Write and Value Help as separate access categories in business roles and SAP also states that, by default, value help access and read access are unrestricted when a business role is created. SAP further explains that value help authorizations do not influence the defined restrictions for read access. This means the restrictions maintained in Maintain Business Roles are part of the authorization concept but SAP does not describe them as a universal mechanism that automatically filters every F4/value help dialog in every app. Based on the current SAP documentation, there is no generic system-wide mechanism that guarantees all unauthorized values are hidden from all value helps

IMPORTANT: Where SAP wants the value help itself to be restricted, SAP delivers this explicitly for the relevant scenario. A clear example is SAP dedicated feature Company Code Restriction for Value Help Dialogs, introduced which is configured via IAM apps of the app authorization variant type (AAVs)

Therefore the behavior you observed for Sales Organization is consistent with standard design a user may still see values in the value help but the restriction is enforced when the application processes the selected value or when the document is checked/saved. In that sense, business role value help restrictions should generally be understood as authorization controls, not as a guaranteed UI filter for every F4 help

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/f65e51a7203443efb58fe535c3d13e5f.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/c926d691d7144f7dba16f8e12ad81d28.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/61f5a1ac89e04b68aaf755c883f1bbac.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/193f9525b71f4dc5a2107597c952b8a9.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: SAP-GUI Single Sign-On based on SSL certificates and SNC
entry.content:
Very good blog. We did all that some time ago. One of the problems is, in SM20 we used to see failed password logins. Now I want to see wrong SNC logins, i.e. when the SNC name does not match, I want to see in some log what was the given SNC name.....

Also - I have to use a modified SM04 transaction - to see SNC logged users info.

entry.title: Re: Field combination not visible when creating new line...
entry.content: The system is working as designed. RES2 appears in delta accruals but not in partial/final settlements when multiple hierarchy levels are involved. No standard configuration exists to change this. Options are either to settle at one hierarchy level or implement a small enhancement to extend RES2 determination logic.

entry.title: Re: Cannot View Software Download, Submit Cases, etc.
entry.content: We finally got this resolved a few weeks ago, it was an invoicing/licensing issue. Took too long for SAP to assist overall IMO.

entry.content:
Withdrawn & Inactive employees are showing in the personnel selection in CAT2.

What is the best way to "hide" these employees so they are not shown when searching in CAT2?

entry.title: Are you prepared to transform your logistics skills before the next project hits?
entry.content:
Returnable packaging is everywhere—logistics, manufacturing, distribution, retail. But managing it efficiently?
Our  brand‑new course  helps you stay ahead and get equipped with real SAP skills  to understand how SAP keeps returnable packaging under control. Access it here 👉 Managing Basic Business Scenarios in SAP Returnable Packaging Management
You will learn

  • Tracking packaging movements across partners
  • Managing deposits, balances, and returns
  • Avoiding losses and inefficiencies
  • Supporting sustainable supply‑chain operations
  • Integrating with core logistics processes in SAP

It’s the foundation anyone needs before diving deeper into SAP's full returnable packaging capabilities.
Perfect for learners who want to:

✔ Build logistics and supply‑chain knowledge
✔ Understand SAP’s sustainability‑focused processes
✔ Strengthen SAP Consulting skills
✔ Support warehouse, inventory, or transportation functions
✔ Grow as part of our SAP Learning community

 

 

Start Exploring the New Course 👉 Jump into the course here

entry.title: PostgreSQL Schema Evolution in SAP CAP - How to Handle DROP and RENAME Operations
entry.content:
I've been working with SAP CAP and @cap-js/postgres , and discovered a critical gap in schema evolution capabilities. CAP's schema_evolution: 'alter' mode intentionally avoids destructive operations (DROP, RENAME) for safety reasons, but this creates real operational challenges in production deployments.

The problem: When you need to remove a column or rename a table, CAP won't handle it automatically. This forces either:
- Manual SQL scripts (error-prone)
- Database downtime for deployments
- Schema drift issues

I've created a solution called @cds-pgroll/postgres  that bridges this gap. It runs as a pre-deployment step using pgroll (inspired by Shopify's approach) to safely handle destructive operations before CAP's standard schema sync.

Key features:
- Automatic CSN delta detection (compares deployed vs. new schema)
- Supports: DROP table/column, RENAME table/column, add constraints
- 2-phase deployment pipeline with Cloud Foundry MTA integration
- Zero-downtime migrations for most operations

If anyone has faced similar challenges with CAP schema evolution, or has feedback on this approach, I'd love to hear your experiences and use cases.

GitHub: https://github.com/Medelkouch98/cds-pgroll
npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@cds-pgroll/postgres

entry.title: Re: Week 2 : Use Joule Chat to create ABAP RAP Application and explore the Explain feature
entry.content: #Challenge completed for Week - 2, Package : ZAC_1620808

entry.title: Re: how to know open posting period by CDS view or API
entry.content:
Hello Lina01,

No, there are no CDSviews of APIs available..
Posting periods in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can only be opened or closed using the Manage Posting Periods app. No API or CDS view exists to perform this operation programmatically.
The system does not have a the posting‑period management logic (OB52 equivalent) through a published API.
Best regards,
Vanessa

entry.title: Re: SAP Job Scheduling Service: We Redesigned the Dashboard (And Added Analytics)
entry.content: @DenisDuev We have one CF space and subaccount for Development, one for Validation and one for Production. All our CAP applications are sharing the same spaces. Developpers are space member is Development space only. As of now, we only have 2 applications that intend to use the JobScheduler service. In development, it is not an issue that all developers can see and manage all applications jobs but it is an issue in production.

entry.title: Accessing Your Financial Data: G/L Account Line Items API
entry.content:
If you're working with SAP's Finance module, the G/L Account Line Items - Read (A2X) API offers a straightforward way to retrieve journal entry information. This service pulls data from the universal journal, giving you access to key accounting fields alongside cost accounting objects.

What You Can Retrieve

The API allows you to access balances across multiple areas including General Ledger Accounting, Asset Accounting, Management Accounting, Margin Analysis, and Material Ledger. You can pull three types of balances:

  • Opening balances show totals from the fiscal year start date up to your selected date, including balance carryforward.
  • Moving balances capture all line items posted within a specific time interval, excluding carried-forward balances—useful for analyzing quarterly movements or custom time periods.
  • Ending balances combine opening balances with changes (credits and debits) within your selected timeframe.

Important Limitations

This API isn't designed for full data extraction. The universal journal contains enormous data volumes, so the service exposes only a subset of fields. We recommend using aggregation and applying strong filters to your requests.

If you need line-item level detail, include the following key fields, for example,

  • AccountingDocument,

  • CompanyCode,

  • FiscalYear,

  • Ledger,

  • LedgerGLLineItem, (ACDOCA line item number)

  • SourceLedger,

Note that AccountingDocumentItem (ACDOCA-BUZEI) is only used for associations to BSEG views.

The API also cannot retrieve cumulative ending balances.

You'll find this service on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, where you can explore example requests and additional documentation to help you get started with your financial data queries. The image shows the API on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

More information

Here's where you find additional useful information:

G/L Account Line Items - Read (A2X)

Communication Management

 APIs in Finance

APIs for General Ledger Accounting

entry.title: Re: Issues in THR81 system based accessment (Successfacto...
entry.content:
Hi @PeterLang ,

I'm sorry to hear that you encountered technical issues during the exam. Maybe that can be taken into account when you submit a support ticket. Especially if you could provide screenshots of those errors. 

Just to give you a heads-up, the support team cannot tell you which tasks you failed as per policy. 

There is a future enhancement to the system-based exam that will show how well you scored on a topic, for example, Configuring Foundation Objects; however, we don't yet know when that will be released. 

Thanks.

entry.title: Transfer event to be used for transferring the data from material determination to settlement doc it
entry.content:
Hi @Lakshmipathi 

We have a requirement to include EAN No. (18 Char) number against each line item in the settlement document. This EAN number comes from material determination setup.

1. Do we have any standard field where we can store this EAN no. so that we can use this EAN no. for report purpose and other purpose. I have seen a field supplier material number at item level (shown in the attached screenshot) can we use this field, i have tried to put some number in the field in manual rebate process to show you but we are using only automatic settlement only (WB2R_SC). Or do we have any other field we have to store this EAN number.

2. Also, Could you please share the transfer event to be used for transferring the data from material determination/Material master to settlement document item level in the transfer manager.

 

Settlement Management SAP S/4HANA
